channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: iPhone 17 Air Rumors: Apple's Thinnest iPhone Yet?
item.description: Apple is reportedly working on a thinner iPhone model, with rumors suggesting it will shave off 2 mm from the iPhone 16 Pro. The iPhone 17 Air is expected to feature Apple's own modems and could be the thinnest iPhone yet.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Musk signals he won't use growing influence to go after competitors
item.description: Elon Musk indicated Friday that he will not use his growing influence in the Trump administration to hurt or seek revenge on his industry competitors.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Appeals court upholds TikTok ban law
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday upheld a law requiring TikTok’s Chinese parent company to sell the popular app or face a U.S. ban. A three-judge panel with the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit found that the law does not violate the First Amendment, as TikTok has argued.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Character.AI is under fire for hosting pro-anorexia chatbots
item.description: “Hello, I am here to make you skinny,” opens the conversation on popular startup Character.AI. “Remember, it won’t be easy, and I won’t accept excuses or failure,” the bot continues.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: China's ban on key high-tech materials could have broad impact on industries, economy
item.description: China has banned exports of key materials used for a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to produce computer chips
item.dc:creator: Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta’s massive $10 billion AI data center is coming to this state
item.description: The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Google introduces PaliGemma 2 vision-language AI models
item.description: Google has introduced a new family of PaliGemma vision-language models, offering scalable performance, long captioning, and support for specialized tasks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From outsider to the Oval Office, bitcoin surges as a new administration embraces crypto
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Born from the ashes of the 2008 global financial crisis, bitcoin arrived after trust had withered in the financial system and in Washington's ability to protect those who must participate in it.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Joby Aviation receives FAA approval for flight academy
item.description: Joby Aviation, the Santa Cruz-based company developing electric air taxis for commercial passenger service, has received certification for its pilot training, in addition to having its voluntary safety management system accepted.
item.dc:creator: James Herrera, The Mercury News

item.title: 8 phishing email scams to watch out for this holiday season
item.description: Be on alert this holiday season for phishing emails. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson shares several examples of fake phishing emails he's received in his inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Landlords are using AI to raise rents; California cities are leading the pushback
item.description: California and federal prosecutors have accused software company RealPage of enriching itself ”at the expense of renters who pay inflated prices.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft: TPM 2.0 is a ‘non-negotiable’ requirement for Windows 11
item.description: With Windows 10 end of support on the horizon, Microsoft said its Trusted Platform Module (TPM) 2.0 requirement for PCs is a “non-negotiable standard” for upgrading to Windows 11.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to bulk delete spam and old text messages on Android
item.description: If unwanted text messages are piling up in your Android inbox, there are some easy steps you can take to clean things up and keep them organized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Get started with Azure AI Content Understanding
item.description: Modern AI is about a lot more than chatbots, as shown by Microsoft’s Ignite 2024 pivot to using its stable of large and small language models to power autonomous agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Bitcoin hits $100K after Trump names pro-crypto SEC chair
item.description: Bitcoin surged to more than $100,000 per coin on Wednesday after President-elect Trump announced Paul Atkins, a cryptocurrency aficionado, would be nominated to head the Security Exchange Commission (SEC). Google valued the coin at 102,726 U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House says at least 8 US telecom firms, dozens of nations impacted by China hacking campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top White House official on Wednesday said at least eight U.S. telecom firms and dozens of nations have been impacted by a Chinese hacking campaign.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said he is “not that worried” about Musk's influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cython tutorial: How to speed up Python
item.description: Python is a powerful programming language that is easy to learn and easy to work with, but it is not always the fastest to run—especially when you’re dealing with math or statistics.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI weapons detection system used in Tennessee schools sued by FTC
item.description: The technology scans students and other visitors for weapons, using AI, without requiring them to remove items from their person, according to previous marketing.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: AT&T sees earnings growth over next 3 years; eyes more than $40B in anticipated shareholder returns
item.description: AT&T anticipates earnings growth over the next three years thanks to the momentum of 5G and fiber services.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KRON4
